summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates host and user info to external endpoint
details: The package's preinstall.js executes on `npm install` and performs an HTTPS request carrying `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo()` data to hackerone.com. Any developer or CI system that installs this package will have their hostname, username, home directory, and uid/gid transmitted to an external host at install time without consent. While hackerone.com is a legitimate security platform (suggesting this may be a bug-bounty researcher's dependency-confusion proof-of-concept against the `@convera` scope), the mechanism — an unsolicited preinstall beacon that leaks installer identity — is the exact shape of a supply-chain attack and produces attacker (or researcher) benefit on every install. The `@convera/ui-shared` name pattern combined with a v0.0.3 beacon-only package is consistent with a namespace-squatting / dependency-confusion probe targeting an internal Convera scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
